summary: Docs-only cleanup removing the `un` / Unknown entry from the `.github/README.md` module abbreviation table. The removed row explicitly said `un` was not an actual folder in the original code, so the PR makes the README's module list better reflect real source/module structure.

key_files.role: README table of short module/folder prefixes; removed the non-real `un` / Unknown entry.

what_changed: Deleted one row from the README module abbreviation table: `` `un`|Unknown|This isn't an actual folder in the original code.``
Left surrounding documented prefixes such as `pl` Player, `sc` Scene, `ty` Toy, and `vi` Visual intact.
smart_moves: Removed a misleading pseudo-module instead of continuing to document an exception that was not backed by an original-code folder.
Kept the change minimal and scoped to the documentation table, with no source or build impact.
decomp_lessons: Keep README/module taxonomy synchronized with actual original-code folders and project structure.
Avoid treating catch-all or historical `Unknown` labels as real modules when they do not correspond to an original folder.
Small documentation cleanups can reduce future naming drift for contributors working from prefix tables.
naming_conventions: README short-prefix table maps compact folder/module codes to full subsystem names, e.g. `pl` = Player, `sc` = Scene, `ty` = Toy, `vi` = Visual.
`un` should not be considered a valid documented module/folder prefix based on this PR.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `un` or `Unknown` module references in docs and source.
Check whether all README module abbreviation entries correspond to actual folders in the original code or repository layout.
Review contributor docs for other historical pseudo-modules that may mislead naming work.
